commands.review_summary:
The PR updates auth-health and gateway model-auth status rollups, with focused tests, so token-type profiles are counted when stale auth order or OAuth-only aggregation would otherwise mark provider auth missing.

PR surface: Source +36, Tests +76. Total +112 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection and the linked issue payload: current main turns an empty effective profile set into provider `missing`, and the gateway OAuth aggregator returns `missing` before token profiles are considered. I did not run a live setup-token migration in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair job is needed; maintainers should wait for CI and decide whether to accept the bounded compatibility-sensitive auth-status semantics.

target.title: fix(auth): count token-type profiles in provider health status rollup
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

claude-cli setup-token users migrating from OAuth get a persistent "auth expired" TUI warning because the provider health rollup ignores token-type credentials when computing effective profiles and dashboard OAuth-status aggregation.
- Problem: Two independent gaps cause token-only credentials to be invisible to auth health: (1) `resolveProviderStatusProfiles` returns empty effective profiles when `explicitOrder` references stale OAuth profile IDs, and (2) `aggregateOAuthStatus` unconditionally returns `missing` when `expectsOAuth` is set but only token-type profiles exist.
- Solution: Fall through to `provider.profiles` when every ordered profile ID is absent from the store (stale/deleted), and aggregate token profile status when no OAuth profiles exist but the provider expects a refreshable credential.
- What changed: `src/agents/auth-health.ts` (resolveProviderStatusProfiles conditional stall-order fallback), `src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.ts` (token status aggregation in aggregateOAuthStatus)
- What did NOT change: OAuth aggregation priority rules, api_key profile handling, auth profile ordering, credential evaluation, dashboard rendering outside aggregateOAuthStatus

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #97996
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

After migrating from OAuth to `claude setup-token`, the TUI permanently shows "Model auth expired — re-authenticate with openclaw models auth" even though the token credential is healthy and the runtime auth route is usable. The provider health summary (`openclaw models status --json`) reports:

```json
{
  "provider": "claude-cli",
  "status": "missing",
  "profiles": [{ "profileId": "claude-cli:setup-token", "type": "token", "status": "ok", ... }],
  "effectiveProfiles": []
}
```

This sends operators toward unnecessary re-authentication and masks real auth failures.

## Evidence

- Behavior addressed: Token-only provider profiles are invisible to auth health rollup when (a) stale explicit auth order references deleted OAuth profiles, and (b) the dashboard OAuth aggregator only checks `type === "oauth"`.
- Real environment tested: Linux x64, Node.js v22.23.0, branch `fix/token-auth-profile-health-97996`

### buildAuthHealthSummary — stale order + token-only profiles

```console
=== Scenario 1: stale order + token-only profiles ===
status: ok
effectiveProfiles: [{"profileId":"claude-cli:token","type":"token","status":"static"}]
```

### buildAuthHealthSummary — incompatible existing profile in order (regression guard)

```console
=== Scenario 2: incompatible existing profile in order ===
status: missing
effectiveProfiles: []
```
(openai:prod exists in store but belongs to openai, not claude-cli — provider stays missing, preserving documented auth.order hard filter.)

### aggregateOAuthStatus — token-only profiles with expectsOAuth

```console
=== aggregateOAuthStatus(token-only, expectsOAuth=true) ===
status: ok
expired token status: expired
```

| Input scenario | Before fix | After fix |
|---|---|---|
| buildAuthHealthSummary: stale order + token-only profiles | `status: "missing"`, `effectiveProfiles: []` | `status: "ok"`, `effectiveProfiles: [{token}]` |
| buildAuthHealthSummary: incompatible existing profile in order | `status: "missing"`, `effectiveProfiles: []` (unchanged) | `status: "missing"`, `effectiveProfiles: []` |
| aggregateOAuthStatus: token-only + expectsOAuth=true | `status: "missing"` | `status: "ok"` |
| aggregateOAuthStatus: expired token + expectsOAuth=true | `status: "missing"` | `status: "expired"` |
| aggregateOAuthStatus: healthy OAuth + expired token | `status: "ok"` (unchanged) | `status: "ok"` |

- Observed result after fix:
  1. Token-only claude-cli providers now show correct auth status instead of `missing`
  2. Token profile health (ok/expired/expiring) is surfaced through the same aggregateOAuthStatus path
  3. Healthy OAuth profiles still correctly mask co-located expired token profiles (existing behavior preserved)
  4. Explicit auth.order targeting an incompatible existing profile still reports `missing` (hard filter preserved)
- What was not tested: Full TUI rendering with a real token-only claude-cli setup; multi-provider auth dashboard pages; keychain-prompt interactions with token credentials

## Root Cause

Two independent invariants were violated:

**Fix 1** — `resolveProviderStatusProfiles` in `src/agents/auth-health.ts`:
When `explicitOrder` references stale profile IDs (e.g., old OAuth profile deleted after migration to setup-token), `resolveAuthProfileOrder` filters them out and returns `[]`. The repair fallback inside `resolveAuthProfileOrder` does not trigger when the order comes from config (vs store). Back in `resolveProviderStatusProfiles`, the `if (explicitOrder)` guard directly returns the empty `explicitOrder.map(...)` result without falling through to `return provider.profiles`. The fix adds a conditional fallback: only when **all** ordered profile IDs are absent from `params.store.profiles` (genuinely stale/deleted) does it fall through to `provider.profiles`; existing but incompatible IDs preserve the documented auth.order hard filter.

**Fix 2** — `aggregateOAuthStatus` in `src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.ts`:
Named "OAuth" but also used for all refreshable-credential providers (token mode). When `expectsOAuth` is true and no OAuth profiles exist, it unconditionally returns `{ status: "missing" }` without checking whether healthy token profiles are present.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- TUI dashboard no longer shows false "auth expired" for claude-cli setup-token users
- `openclaw models status --json` correctly reports token-only provider health
- Explicit auth.order semantics preserved: incompatible existing profile IDs still report missing
- No config changes required; no migration needed

## Security Impact (required)

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: stale explicitOrder + token-only profiles → effectiveProfiles non-empty, status ok; token-only + expectsOAuth → ok; expired token → expired; incompatible existing profile in order → missing (hard filter preserved)
- Edge cases checked: mixed OAuth+token (OAuth wins), no profiles, stale order from config vs store, existing profile from different provider in order
- What you did NOT verify: Full gateway runtime with real claude-cli setup-token workflow

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes — the narrowed fallback only activates for genuinely stale/deleted profile IDs, preserving the existing hard-filter behavior for incompatible profiles
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes — repairs the two narrow gaps (stale-order conditional fallthrough + token status aggregation) at the shared utility boundary, keeping both fixes in the functions that already own auth-health rollup logic. The conditional fallback (allOrderedIdsAbsent guard) addresses the ClawSweeper P1 review finding by preserving documented auth.order filtering for incompatible existing profiles.
- **Refactor needed**: No. The token aggregation inside `aggregateOAuthStatus` follows the same priority rules as the existing OAuth block. A broader rename or signature change would unnecessarily touch callers in model-auth status and TUI dashboard without changing behavior.
- **Alternative considered**: Renaming `aggregateOAuthStatus` → `aggregateRefreshableCredentialStatus`. Rejected because it would be a pure refactor with zero behavioral change, increasing diff size and review cost without benefit.

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Co-Authored-By**: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Highest risk area**: Compatibility for providers that rely on `aggregateOAuthStatus` to return `missing` when no OAuth profiles exist, now receiving a token-derived status instead. Mitigated because the token aggregation path mirrors the OAuth priority rules exactly and only activates when `expectsOAuth` is true AND no OAuth profiles exist.
- **Compatibility**: Non-breaking. Token profiles that were previously invisible in `effectiveProfiles` become visible only when explicit auth.order IDs are genuinely absent from the store. Existing incompatible profile IDs preserve the hard filter.
- **Edge case**: If a provider has both OAuth (expired) and token (ok) profiles, OAuth still wins the status aggregation (no change). If explicitOrder names a profile ID that exists in the store but for a different provider, the provider stays missing (hard filter preserved).

---

Related to #97996
target.author: sunnyshu0925
